title: Griots Fellowship 2026 for African Storytellers: Apply Now for the LéO Africa Institute and KAS Uganda Program
summary: The LéO Africa Institute, in partnership with Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung (KAS) Uganda, has officially opened applications for the Griots Fellowship 2026, a unique opportunity for African storytellers, creatives, and cultural practitioners to amplify their voices and shape Africa’s narrative. Inspired by the rich West African tradition of the Griot—the keeper of memory, community voice, and cultural identity—the fellowship is designed for individuals who believe storytelling is a powerful tool for reclaiming Africa’s place in the global conversation. Now entering its second cohort, the Griots Fellowship is seeking practitioners with compelling stories to tell, meaningful projects to develop, and the courage to share their voices with the world. What is the Griots Fellowship? The Griots Fellowship is a leadership and storytelling development program created for writers, filmmakers, podcasters, musicians, visual artists, oral historians, and other cultural practitioners across Africa. The fellowship combines storytelling, leadership, media ethics, and public speaking to help fellows refine their craft and build lasting impact through their narratives. Participants will engage in a transformative journey that culminates in delivering a powerful TED-style presentation known as The LéO Africa Talk before a live audience. 2026 Cohort Theme “Stories that will Define Our Future” This year’s theme focuses on the narratives that will shape Africa’s next chapter—stories of identity, resilience, innovation, leadership, heritage, and transformation. Fellows will explore how their personal and community experiences can contribute to defining the continent’s future. Fellowship Program Areas Selected fellows will go through four key stages of development: 1. Narrative Strategy Craft Participants will sharpen their storytelling skills, strengthen their message, and refine the purpose behind their work. 2. Media Production Ethics The fellowship explores responsibility, representation, and integrity in storytelling, helping fellows understand the ethical dimensions of media creation. 3. Platform Building Fellows will learn how to expand their visibility, build audiences, and create platforms that give their work greater reach and influence. 4. The LéO Africa Talk Each fellow will prepare and deliver a carefully crafted TED-style talk before a live audience, showcasing their story and perspective. Who Should Apply? Applications are open to: Emerging leaders aged 25 to 45 Individuals based anywhere on the African continent Writers, artists, filmmakers, podcasters, musicians, oral historians, and cultural practitioners Storytellers with a personal or community-rooted story they are committed to bringing to life Whether your work is in literature, film, visual arts, music, oral storytelling, or digital media, this fellowship offers a powerful platform for your voice to be heard. Why You Should Apply The Griots Fellowship offers more than training—it provides a space for reflection, visibility, and influence. Benefits include: Professional storytelling and leadership development Mentorship and guidance from experienced practitioners Public speaking and presentation training Increased visibility through The LéO Africa Talk A network of changemakers and storytellers across Africa A chance to shape the narratives that define the continent’s future How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application link below: Apply Here Deadline is 30th April 2026 Africa’s future will be shaped by the stories its people choose to tell. The Griots Fellowship 2026 is an invitation for bold storytellers to step forward, own their narratives, and influence the continent’s cultural and intellectual future. If you have a story that deserves to be heard, this is your opportunity. Discover similar opportunities Here. The post Griots Fellowship 2026 for African Storytellers: Apply Now for the LéO Africa Institute and KAS Uganda Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Tony Elumelu Foundation Careers 2026: Brand Officer, Research Manager & M&E Officer Roles in Lagos, Nigeria
summary: The Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF) , Africa’s leading philanthropy dedicated to empowering entrepreneurs, is currently recruiting for multiple strategic roles in Lagos, Nigeria. These positions— Brand Officer, Research Manager, and Monitoring Evaluation (M E) Officer —offer professionals the opportunity to contribute directly to Africa’s economic transformation through entrepreneurship, research, and impact-driven initiatives. Founded in 2010 by African investor and philanthropist Tony O. Elumelu, TEF has committed over $100 million to empowering African entrepreneurs. Through its flagship programmes, partnerships, and digital platforms such as TEFConnect, the Foundation has supported over 9,000 entrepreneurs across 54 African countries and reached over 1 million individuals. These career opportunities are ideal for professionals passionate about development, entrepreneurship, policy, research, and strategic communications. About the Tony Elumelu Foundation (TEF) The Tony Elumelu Foundation is a pan-African organization focused on: Driving entrepreneurship across Africa Promoting private-sector-led economic growth Reducing poverty and unemployment Supporting innovation and inclusive development Through partnerships with global organizations such as the EU, UNDP, and the Red Cross, TEF continues to expand its impact and strengthen Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem. Available Job Positions 1. Brand Officer (Lagos, Nigeria) Job Purpose The Brand Officer plays a critical role in shaping and maintaining TEF’s brand identity across all platforms. This role ensures that the Foundation’s messaging, storytelling, and visual communication reflect its mission and reinforce its leadership in African entrepreneurship. Key Responsibilities Brand Management Strategy Execute TEF’s brand strategy across communication channels Maintain brand consistency across all materials Protect and enhance brand reputation Ensure alignment with TEF’s identity and values Marketing, Communications Storytelling Develop brand-led campaigns and content Support programme launches and communication initiatives Create reports, presentations, and digital assets Amplify impact stories across Africa Graphic Design Visual Content Design digital and print materials (social media, reports, flyers) Translate brand strategy into compelling visuals Maintain visual consistency across platforms Digital Media Support Manage brand presence across digital platforms Monitor media coverage and brand performance Support reporting and analytics Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate with internal teams and external partners Coordinate with agencies and vendors Promote internal brand awareness Qualifications Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Branding, or related field Minimum of 5 years’ experience Strong storytelling and branding expertise Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills Proficiency in digital and social media platforms Learn More Here 2. Research Manager – Africapitalism Institute (Lagos, Nigeria) Job Purpose The Research Manager will lead the revival and strategic direction of the Africapitalism Institute, positioning it as a leading research and thought leadership platform across Africa. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Lead the Africapitalism Institute Develop long-term research agendas Build partnerships with universities and institutions Research Design Execution Conduct high-quality research on entrepreneurship and economic development Design quantitative and qualitative methodologies Data Analysis Analyze large datasets using econometric and statistical tools Generate insights for policymakers and stakeholders Thought Leadership Produce policy papers, research reports, and publications Support global academic and policy engagement Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate with governments, think tanks, and development partners Drive Africapitalism discourse globally Qualifications Requirements Master’s degree (PhD preferred) Minimum of 10 years’ research experience Strong publication record Advanced data analysis skills (Stata, SPSS, etc.) Experience with policy engagement and academic research Learn More Here 3. Monitoring Evaluation (M E) Officer (Lagos, Nigeria) Job Purpose The M E Officer will support the design and implementation of monitoring systems to evaluate TEF’s programmes across Africa, ensuring effective impact measurement and learning. Key Responsibilities Monitoring Evaluation Systems Develop and implement M E frameworks across 54 countries Conduct impact assessments and data collection Data Management Reporting Maintain centralized data systems Conduct quarterly data quality assessments Learning Knowledge Sharing Capture lessons learned and best practices Develop case studies and impact stories Collaboration Work with communications and programme teams Support advocacy and campaign strategies Qualifications Requirements Master’s degree Minimum of 5 years’ experience in M E Strong data analysis and reporting skills Experience with tools such as Excel, SPSS, BI tools Ability to work in multicultural environments Learn More Here Core Competencies Across Roles Strong alignment with TEF’s mission and values Passion for entrepreneurship and development High attention to detail Ability to work in fast-paced, multicultural environments Strong collaboration and accountability Why Work at the Tony Elumelu Foundation? Working at TEF offers: Opportunity to impact millions of entrepreneurs across Africa Exposure to international partnerships and development programs Career growth in a high-impact, mission-driven organization Participation in one of Africa’s largest entrepreneurial ecosystems Access to global networks in development, policy, and research Location All roles are based in: Lagos, Nigeria Application Details Application deadlines for all three positions is April 30th, 2026. Candidates should ensure all required qualifications and experience are met before applying Candidates are to send all application materials to careers@heirsholdings.com before the deadline. Conclusion The Tony Elumelu Foundation’s 2026 recruitment presents a unique opportunity for professionals to contribute to Africa’s transformation through entrepreneurship, research, and development impact. Whether you are a branding expert, a seasoned researcher, or an M E specialist, these roles provide a platform to drive meaningful change at scale across the continent. Click HERE to discover more global Job opportunities on OFY. title: AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 | AmaliTech | AI-Powered Technology National Service Opportunity in Ghana
summary: The AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 is a structured, technology-focused national service opportunity designed for Ghanaian graduates who are eligible for the 2026/2027 National Service year and are interested in building a practical, AI-powered tech career . This National Service Programme combines hands-on industry experience, mentorship, and exposure to international project teams, offering participants a strong foundation for long-term careers in software, data, and digital transformation roles. Organised by AmaliTech , a leading technology services company with a strong focus on talent development in Africa, the AmaliTech National Service Programme goes beyond traditional National Service placements. The programme integrates real-world project work , structured learning pathways, and professional development within an AI-enabled environment that prepares participants for employment beyond the National Service Scheme (NSS). Overview of the AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 The AmaliTech National Service Programme is tailored for graduates who want to apply their academic knowledge in real technology environments while serving their mandatory national service. Participants are trained and deployed across specialised technology pathways, supported by mentors, modern tools, and structured assessments. The programme places strong emphasis on software development, digital transformation, data engineering, and systems engineering , ensuring that participants graduate from the programme with both technical expertise and workplace readiness. Specialisation Pathways Participants in the AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 are placed into one of the following specialisation pathways , depending on their background, interest, and assessment outcomes: Digital Transformation This pathway focuses on building cross-functional business and technology skills . Participants develop competencies in product ownership, project management, consulting, and business analysis. The goal is to prepare graduates to work closely with business leaders to drive value through technology-enabled solutions. Experience Engineering The Experience Engineering pathway is centred on designing and delivering high-quality digital products . It combines design thinking with technical skills in UI/UX design, frontend development, mobile application development, and quality assurance. Participants also leverage modern AI tools to build, test, and validate user-centred digital solutions. Systems Engineering This pathway equips participants with strong architectural foundations and specialised technical skills in backend development, cloud computing, cybersecurity, DevOps, and test automation engineering. Learners are trained to design secure and scalable systems, orchestrate Agentic AI solutions, and protect mission-critical platforms. Data Engineering Analytics The Data Engineering Analytics pathway provides end-to-end exposure to data platforms. Participants gain skills in data engineering, business intelligence, and machine learning, enabling them to support responsible, AI-driven insights and data-informed decision-making across organisations. Eligibility Criteria Must-Have Requirements Applicants must meet all of the following criteria to be eligible: Must be eligible for National Service for the 2026/2027 service year Must have a major or minor in: Computer Science Computer Engineering Information Technology (IT) Management Information Systems (MIS) Business Informatics Must have some experience in IT consulting, software engineering, or data engineering Must demonstrate a strong passion for a career in IT consulting, software engineering, or data engineering Exceptional Applicants Applicants from other educational backgrounds may also apply if they possess relevant professional experience or recognised certifications related to technology, software, or data fields. The Hecker Fellowship (Top Performer Track) Outstanding participants in the AmaliTech National Service Programme may be selected for the Hecker Fellowship , an exclusive track designed for top-performing NSS trainees. The fellowship accelerates career growth and provides enhanced leadership exposure. Benefits of the Hecker Fellowship include: Direct mentorship from the CEO Sign-on bonus Executive-level exposure and career acceleration opportunities Why Join AmaliTech for National Service? Full-Time Employment Opportunities Participants who excel during the programme may be offered full-time junior engineer positions at AmaliTech, with competitive compensation and opportunities for rapid career advancement. Real-World Industry Experience Participants work on international project teams , gaining exposure to global standards, tools, and collaborative workflows. Financial and Technical Support The programme provides: A competitive monthly stipend A complete workstation, including a laptop, monitor, and headphones High-speed internet access and required software licenses Career Guidance and Professional Development Participants benefit from structured career guidance, networking opportunities, and training in cross-cultural communication, interview preparation, and workplace effectiveness. Company Culture The programme includes social activities, team-building events, and end-of-year bonus packages that promote a positive and supportive company culture. Application Process The AmaliTech National Service Programme application process consists of four structured stages: Application and Aptitude Test All applicants complete a multiple-choice assessment covering abstract reasoning, verbal reasoning, and numerical reasoning. Specialist Assessment Candidates complete a technical assessment related to their selected specialisation pathway. Take-Home Lab and Interviews Interviews are based on the lab submission and assess technical depth, leadership potential, communication skills, and collaboration ability. Offer to Shortlisted Candidates Successful candidates receive an offer to complete their National Service with AmaliTech in Accra, Kumasi, or Takoradi . Important Dates Service Year: 2026/2027 National Service period Application timelines and assessment stages follow the official AmaliTech recruitment schedule via the application portal Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully follow all instructions provided on the official website. How to Apply Interested and qualified applicants should complete the online application by clicking the link below and following all instructions provided on the application portal. APPLY HERE The AmaliTech National Service Programme 2026/2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for Ghanaian graduates to complete their National Service while gaining practical, AI-powered technology experience , professional mentorship, and exposure to international projects. With structured specialisation pathways, strong career support, and potential full-time employment opportunities, this National Service Programme stands out as a strategic pathway into long-term careers in software, data, and digital transformation. title: Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026: Apply for UNDP and Sierra Leone Youth Driver EV Programme
summary: The Ministry of Youth Affairs in Sierra Leone, in collaboration with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), has officially opened applications for the “Sierra Ride” Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026 , a pilot programme designed to modernize the country’s commercial transport sector while creating new economic opportunities for young drivers. This innovative project introduces electric vehicles (EVs) into Sierra Leone’s transport system to reduce carbon emissions, improve urban mobility, and promote green jobs for youth across the country. About the Sierra Ride Initiative The Sierra Ride Initiative is part of Sierra Leone’s broader national strategy to transform urban transportation through sustainable and climate-friendly mobility solutions. The programme aims to replace traditional fuel-powered commercial vehicles—especially the widely used three-wheelers known locally as kekes —with environmentally friendly electric alternatives. Traditional kekes consume significant amounts of gasoline and contribute heavily to urban air pollution. By transitioning to electric vehicles, Sierra Leone hopes to: Reduce greenhouse gas emissions Improve urban air quality Lower transport operating costs Promote road safety Support climate-smart innovation Create sustainable youth employment opportunities The initiative is co-financed and implemented by the Government of Sierra Leone with support from: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) Global Environment Facility (GEF) European Union through the SOLUTIONSPlus Project Local transport and environmental stakeholders Who Can Apply? The programme is specifically targeting young Sierra Leoneans aged 21 to 35 years who are interested in becoming part of the country’s growing green transport sector. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be between 21 and 35 years old Have at least 3 years of driving experience Possess a valid driver’s licence Have a clean driving record Must have completed Senior Secondary Education Provide a Police Clearance Certificate Show willingness to undergo training on electric vehicle technology Special Inclusion Priority The Ministry of Youth Affairs strongly encourages applications from: Women Persons with disabilities This aligns with the government’s commitment to promoting gender equality and inclusive economic empowerment in the transport industry. Why Sierra Ride Matters During the 2025 International Youth Day celebrations, Minister of Youth Affairs Ibrahim Sannoh announced a youth empowerment package that included the rollout of 50 electric vehicles , with special priority for women drivers. The minister emphasized that promoting climate technology, innovation, and electric mobility is a major national priority. This initiative also builds on UNDP’s recent efforts to empower young women with technical and vocational skills. Earlier in 2026, UNDP partnered with NAYCOM and AFTEC to certify 250 young women in fields such as: Auto mechanics Renewable energy systems Technical vocational skills The Sierra Ride project now creates a direct pathway for young people—especially women—to apply these skills in real-world income-generating opportunities. Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will benefit from: Access to electric commercial vehicles Training on electric vehicle operations and maintenance Entry into the growing green transport economy Reduced fuel and operating costs Opportunities for sustainable income generation Participation in Sierra Leone’s climate-smart mobility transition How to Apply Interested applicants should submit the following documents: Updated CV Cover Letter Copy of Driver’s Licence Submission Options Applications can be submitted via: Email info@moya.gov.sl Physical Submission Ministry of Youth Affairs 24 Scan Drive Wilberforce, Freetown Sierra Leone Application Deadline Deadline: 8th May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute delays. Visit Here , for more details. The Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026 offers more than just a driving opportunity—it is a gateway into Sierra Leone’s green economy and a bold step toward sustainable urban transportation. For young drivers seeking employment, empowerment, and the chance to be part of a climate-friendly future, this programme is an excellent opportunity to accelerate both career growth and national development. If you meet the eligibility criteria, this is the perfect time to take the wheel and drive toward a greener future. Discover similar opportunities Here The post Sierra Ride Electric Vehicle Initiative 2026: Apply for UNDP and Sierra Leone Youth Driver EV Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Canada LIFTs Program 2026: Transformative Funding & Learning Opportunity for Small and Medium-Sized Organizations
summary: The Canada LIFTs Program is an innovative and purpose-driven initiative designed to empower small and medium-sized organizations (SMOs) based in Canada and their global partners. This program goes beyond traditional funding models by combining financial support with capacity-building resources, collaborative learning, and meaningful peer engagement. At its core, Canada LIFTs seeks to reshape international cooperation by reducing systemic barriers, redistributing power, and strengthening the broader development ecosystem in which SMOs operate. What is the Canada LIFTs Program? Canada LIFTs is a comprehensive funding and learning platform aimed at supporting community-led international development initiatives. It recognizes the critical role that smaller organizations play in driving impactful, grassroots change and provides them with the tools and resources needed to scale their efforts effectively. Key Objectives of Canada LIFTs The program is structured around several transformative goals: Reduce Barriers to Access Simplify funding processes for smaller organizations Provide equitable opportunities for underrepresented groups Shift Power Dynamics Promote locally led development initiatives Encourage decision-making at the community level Strengthen Organizational Ecosystems Build long-term sustainability for SMOs Foster collaboration across organizations and regions Program Components Canada LIFTs stands out due to its integrated approach, combining multiple forms of support: Funding Opportunities Direct financial support through dedicated funding streams Learning Resources Access to tools, guides, and training for organizational growth Peer Networking Opportunities to connect with like-minded organizations and partners Capacity Building Strengthening leadership, strategy, and operational capabilities Available Funding Streams Applicants can register for one of the following funds: Canada LIFTs Momentum Fund Designed to accelerate existing initiatives and scale impact Canada LIFTs Together Fund Focused on fostering partnerships and collaborative projects Application Details Application Period: April 1 – June 30, 2026 Deadline: June 30, 2026, at 1:00 PM Central Time Registration Requirement: Applicants must register within the application window to be considered Why You Should Apply Participating in Canada LIFTs offers numerous advantages: Access to flexible and purpose-driven funding Opportunities to collaborate with global partners Enhanced organizational skills and strategic capacity Inclusion in a supportive and innovative development network Ability to amplify community-led impact on an international scale Who Should Consider This Program? Canada LIFTs is particularly beneficial for organizations that: Are small to medium-sized and based in Canada Work in international development or community-led initiatives Seek to expand their reach and effectiveness Value collaboration, innovation, and equity in development work How to Get Started Interested organizations should prepare to register for either the Momentum Fund or the Together Fund before the deadline. Early preparation is encouraged to ensure eligibility and a strong application. For more information about this opportunity, click here . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . title: Apply Now: ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Start-up Competition for Climate Tech and Green Innovation (Global Opportunity)
summary: Are you building a climate-focused startup or have a powerful green business idea that could help shape a more sustainable future? Applications for ClimateLaunchpad 2026 are now open, offering early-stage entrepreneurs the chance to access expert mentorship, investor networks, global exposure, and the opportunity to compete on the international stage. Since 2014, ClimateLaunchpad has grown into the world’s largest green business ideas competition , operating across 97 countries and helping more than 5,000 teams transform climate innovations into impactful ventures. Best of all, the programme is completely free to join , with no participation fees. About ClimateLaunchpad ClimateLaunchpad is more than just a competition—it is a global platform dedicated to helping entrepreneurs build solutions for climate change and sustainability challenges. The programme supports innovators by providing the expertise, validation, and business development skills needed to turn early-stage climate ideas into scalable businesses. Over the years, ClimateLaunchpad has delivered: More than 500 training bootcamps A strong international network of investors and partners Over €500 million raised in investment by alumni startups The initiative continues to empower founders who are creating practical solutions for environmental sustainability, clean energy, circular economy, food systems, water management, and other climate-related sectors. What’s in It for You? Selected participants in the 2026 ClimateLaunchpad cohort will get the opportunity to: 1. Receive Expert Coaching and Mentorship Work with experienced coaches and mentors who will help refine your idea and strengthen your business strategy. 2. Validate Your Business Model Join a structured programme designed to test assumptions, improve your market fit, and sharpen your startup model. 3. Connect with Global Investors Gain access to a powerful international network of investors, industry experts, and fellow changemakers. 4. Pitch at the Global Grand Final in Singapore Top-performing startups will advance to the Global Grand Final in Singapore , where they will pitch live and compete for major prizes and recognition. ClimateLaunchpad 2026 Programme Structure The programme follows a step-by-step development journey: Step 0 – Start Learning: Mini-Course Participants begin with foundational learning resources to prepare for the programme. Step 1 – Build Your Idea: Boot Camp A practical training bootcamp focused on developing and strengthening your climate startup idea. Step 2 – Test and Refine: Follow-Up Coaching and National Finals Receive additional support while preparing for national-level pitching competitions. Step 3 – Compete Internationally: Regional Finals Top startups move forward to regional competitions. Step 4 – The Global Stage: Global Grand Final The best teams compete globally at the final international event in Singapore. Eligibility Requirements To apply for ClimateLaunchpad 2026, your sustainability venture must meet the following conditions: Your startup must be less than two years old You must have raised less than €200,000 in total funding You must not yet have sold products or services on commercial terms If your startup meets these criteria, you are eligible to apply. Application Deadline Deadline: 15 May 2026 (23:59 CET) Please note that deadlines may vary depending on your country, so applicants are strongly encouraged to check their local application timeline on the official website. How to Apply Interested founders should: Visit the official ClimateLaunchpad website for country-specific details Confirm local deadlines and programme information Submit their application through the official application portal ClimateLaunchpad 2026 is an incredible opportunity for early-stage climate entrepreneurs who want to transform innovative ideas into real-world solutions. If you are passionate about sustainability and ready to scale your climate startup, this global competition could provide the mentorship, visibility, and funding opportunities needed to take your venture to the next level. With expert guidance, investor access, and a pathway to the Global Grand Final in Singapore, this is one opportunity green innovators should not miss. Discover similar Grant opportunity Here. title: WISE Internship 2026 in Europe: Fully Funded Western Balkans Internship Scheme for Young Professionals (Apply by May 4)
summary: The Young Professionals Network (YPN), with support from the German Foreign Office , has launched an exciting and transformative opportunity for emerging leaders through the Western Balkans Young Professionals Internship Scheme for Empowerment (WISE) 2026 . This fully funded internship program is designed to empower young professionals across the Western Balkans by equipping them with practical experience, cross-border exposure, and critical leadership skills needed to thrive in today’s global workforce. If you are looking to gain international work experience, build a strong professional network, and contribute to regional development, this opportunity is tailored for you. Application Deadline The application period runs from April 16 to May 4, 2026 . What is the WISE Internship Scheme? The WISE Internship Scheme is a first-of-its-kind regional initiative that aims to address: Youth unemployment Skills gaps in the labor market Limited workforce mobility Weak regional economic cooperation It aligns with major regional frameworks such as: The Common Regional Market The Berlin Process The EU Single Market Through this program, selected participants will engage in three-month internships across the Western Balkans, gaining hands-on experience while contributing to innovation and collaboration across borders. Program Overview Duration: 3 months Participants: 12 selected candidates Countries Covered: 6 Western Balkans countries Fields: Technology Communication Green jobs Civic engagement Key Objectives of WISE The program is structured around five core pillars: 1. Networking Build a strong network of young professionals Foster collaboration across borders 2. Mobility Enable participants to live and work in a different country within the region Enhance adaptability and global exposure 3. Cooperation Strengthen economic ties between businesses and institutions Promote regional integration 4. Skills Development Align participants’ skills with labor market demands Improve employability 5. Innovation Encourage creative solutions to regional challenges Support broader development agendas Why You Should Apply for WISE 2026 1. Hands-On Work Experience Work with leading businesses and NGOs Gain real-world exposure in high-demand sectors 2. Professional Skills Training Participate in a 4-day intensive training program in Albania Focus areas: Leadership Workplace ethics Intercultural communication 3. International Mobility Live and work in a different Western Balkans country Experience diverse cultures and work environments 4. Strong Networking Opportunities Connect with professionals, mentors, and peers Build long-term career relationships 5. Fully Funded Internship Monthly stipends provided Travel and accommodation costs covered 6. Certification Receive a professional certificate upon completion Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the WISE Internship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: 1. Age Requirement Must be between 20 and 30 years old 2. Educational Background Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree Final-year students or recent graduates may apply with proof of completion 3. Nationality Must be a citizen of one of the Western Balkans countries: Albania Bosnia and Herzegovina Kosovo Montenegro North Macedonia Serbia 4. Experience Minimum of two years of professional experience or: Internship experience Community engagement Leadership involvement 5. Language Requirement Must be fluent in English 6. Personal Qualities Strong motivation and integrity Ability to work independently Openness to learning and collaboration 7. Personal Statement Must demonstrate alignment with WISE goals Show interest in regional cooperation and development Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide Step 1: Submit Application Applicants must provide: CV Recommendation letter Academic credentials Personal statement Step 2: Screening Phase (May 5 – May 10, 2026) Applications are reviewed to ensure eligibility Step 3: Interview Stage (May 11 – May 13, 2026) Shortlisted candidates are invited for interviews Focus on: Skills Motivation Career goals Step 4: Matching Process (May 2026) Candidates are paired with host organizations Matching is based on: Skills Interests Organizational needs Step 5: Final Selection Notification Selected applicants receive confirmation and next steps Important Note The use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) is strictly prohibited during the application process All submissions must be original Any form of plagiarism will lead to disqualification Program Journey Beyond Selection Selected participants will go through: Pre-departure orientation Internship placement Ongoing mentorship and support Networking and collaboration activities Final certification upon completion How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official WISE application portal. For inquiries, contact: network@ypn.al APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The WISE Internship Scheme 2026 is more than just an internship—it is a career-launching platform designed to shape the next generation of leaders in the Western Balkans. With full funding, international exposure, and structured development, this program offers a rare opportunity to gain both professional and personal growth while contributing to regional progress. For more information on this INTERNSHIP VISIT HERE For more internship offers, visit OFY HERE The post WISE Internship 2026 in Europe: Fully Funded Western Balkans Internship Scheme for Young Professionals (Apply by May 4) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for FTPF Orchard Donation 2026: Get Free Fruit Trees for Schools, Nonprofits and Communities
summary: Communities across the United States now have an incredible opportunity to receive free fruit trees and orchard support through the Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF). This award-winning international nonprofit is inviting eligible organizations to apply for orchard donations that help fight hunger, improve the environment, and strengthen local communities. If your school, nonprofit, public park, or community garden is looking to create a lasting source of fresh fruit and environmental education, this program could be the perfect fit. About the Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF) The Fruit Tree Planting Foundation (FTPF) is a globally recognized nonprofit charity dedicated to planting fruitful trees and plants to address some of the world’s biggest challenges. Their mission includes: Alleviating world hunger Combating global warming Strengthening communities Improving air quality Enhancing soil and water health FTPF strategically donates orchards where the harvest can serve people for generations. Their orchards are commonly planted in: Community gardens Public schools City and state parks Low-income neighborhoods Native American reservations Hunger relief sites Animal sanctuaries The goal is to ensure that fresh fruit remains accessible while creating educational and environmental benefits for the community. What FTPF Provides Organizations selected for an orchard donation receive far more than just trees. FTPF offers a complete orchard support package, including: High-quality fruit trees and shrubs Planting equipment On-site orchard design expertise Professional oversight during planting Horticultural workshops Aftercare training and manuals Subsidized deer fencing where needed Drip irrigation support when necessary Volunteer coordination assistance Educational planting-day experiences For schools, FTPF also provides fun, age-appropriate environmental education programs that teach students about: The importance of trees Healthy fruit consumption Sustainability and environmental stewardship This makes the orchard project both practical and educational. Who Can Apply? FTPF orchard donations are available to organizations that can demonstrate a strong commitment to caring for the trees and using the orchard for charitable purposes. Eligible applicants include: Nonprofits NGOs Public schools Government entities Important Note for International Applicants At the moment, FTPF is not accepting applications from outside the United States because their international programming is currently at capacity. Applications submitted from outside the U.S. will not be processed at this time. FTPF Orchard Application Requirements To qualify, applicants must meet the following requirements: 1. Serve a Charitable Purpose Applicants must be a nonprofit, NGO, public school, or government entity working toward a charitable mission. 2. Long-Term Commitment to Tree Care Recipients must commit to maintaining the orchard permanently and using it to support their charitable goals. 3. Suitable Planting Site Applicants must have enough horticulturally appropriate land to host a grove of fruit trees planted about 15 feet apart. Most programs require approximately: 15–20 trees minimum The FTPF application helps assess whether your site is suitable. 4. Land Ownership or Long-Term Access Applicants must either: Own the planting site Have a long-term lease Partner with a nonprofit or government entity that owns the site with a long-term usage agreement 5. Reliable Irrigation Access The orchard site must have a dependable year-round water source nearby. 6. Volunteer Participation Recipients must help coordinate local volunteers for planting day, as community involvement improves long-term orchard stewardship. Why This Opportunity Matters An FTPF orchard is more than a tree donation—it is a long-term investment in food security, environmental sustainability, and community empowerment. Whether you manage a school, a nonprofit, or a public community space, this opportunity can create lasting impact for generations to come. If your organization is ready to grow fresh food, engage volunteers, and improve your local environment, now is the perfect time to apply for an FTPF orchard. Application Deadline There is no fixed deadline for FTPF orchard applications. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis , meaning organizations can apply at any time. However, FTPF notes that their schedule is currently near capacity, so response times may be slow. Applicants should be patient, as worthy projects are often considered when suitable opportunities arise. Once submitted: You will receive an acknowledgment letter Your application remains permanently in FTPF’s database There is no need to reapply if you do not hear back immediately How to Apply Interested organizations should: Step 1: Review the Requirements Carefully confirm that your organization meets all eligibility criteria. Step 2: Download the FTPF Application Form Download the official application form from the FTPF website. Step 3: Submit via Email Send your completed application form to: Email: info@ftpf.org Discover similar Grant Opportunities Here. The post Apply for FTPF Orchard Donation 2026: Get Free Fruit Trees for Schools, Nonprofits and Communities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AI in the Newsroom Fellowship 2026 for African Journalists: Fully Funded Opportunity by Africa Uncensored and DW Akademie
summary: Africa Uncensored, in partnership with DW Akademie, has officially opened applications for the AI in the Newsroom Fellowship 2026 , a six-month intensive fellowship designed to help journalists bridge the gap between journalism and emerging technologies. This unique program is built for media professionals who want to go beyond simply using AI tools for basic tasks and instead develop practical, newsroom-ready solutions that improve editorial workflows and reporting processes. Running from June to December 2026 , the fellowship offers selected participants the opportunity to design, test, and implement AI-powered products tailored to their newsroom challenges. About the AI in the Newsroom Fellowship 2026 The AI in the Newsroom Fellowship is not just another AI training program focused on prompting chatbots. It is a hands-on technical laboratory for journalists who want to build real solutions using artificial intelligence. Participants will work on creating tools for adoption in their newsrooms while also developing customized AI workflows for editorial processes. Whether you are already experimenting with AI-assisted journalism or just beginning to explore how AI can improve your reporting, this fellowship provides the mentorship, technical support, and practical environment needed to make your ideas a reality. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will gain access to several valuable opportunities, including: 1. Technical Upskilling Participants will receive deep training in: AI tools for data journalism AI literacy and responsible AI use AI-assisted editorial workflows Technical guidance from AI experts and practitioners 2. Project Mentorship Fellows will receive dedicated mentorship to help them move from identifying a newsroom bottleneck to building a polished and deployable solution. 3. Collaborative Network Participants will join a strong cohort of journalists, editors, and media innovators committed to advancing journalism through technology. 4. Resource Access Fellows will gain access to: Dedicated AI sandbox tools Frameworks for testing newsroom solutions Support systems for solving emerging editorial challenges Who Should Apply? The fellowship is open to: Journalists Editors News content creators Media professionals interested in AI-supported journalism Applicants must be able to define a clear use case explaining how they plan to use AI tools to solve a specific newsroom challenge. Each fellow must identify a concrete problem within their newsroom and demonstrate how their proposed AI prototype will solve it. Applicants are also required to submit: A letter of commitment from their newsroom Or, for freelancers, a personal letter of commitment for the proposed project Ideal Candidate Profile You are an ideal candidate if: You clearly understand your newsroom challenges and how AI can help solve them You are comfortable experimenting with new tools and approaches You have a “hacker mindset” toward problem-solving You are committed to participating throughout the full fellowship cycle (June–December 2026) You want to responsibly adopt AI-augmented reporting tools in journalism Fellowship Timeline Important Dates Information Session: Mid-April 2026 Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 Fellowship Start Date: June 2026 Program Conclusion: 31 December 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application form provided by Africa Uncensored. The organiser are particularly looking for projects that prioritise: Transparency Financial accountability Public interest journalism Applicants are advised not to send applications via email, as only submissions made through the official form will be considered. APPLY HERE Please note that this email is strictly for inquiries only and not for submitting applications. Final Thoughts The AI in the Newsroom Fellowship 2026 presents a powerful opportunity for African journalists to future-proof their careers while building innovative solutions for their newsrooms. As AI continues to transform the global media landscape, this fellowship offers the perfect platform to learn, experiment, and lead the next generation of responsible AI-powered journalism. If you are ready to move beyond theory and build real newsroom innovation, this fellowship is for you. Discover similar Fellowship opportunities Here. The post AI in the Newsroom Fellowship 2026 for African Journalists: Fully Funded Opportunity by Africa Uncensored and DW Akademie appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program (JJ/WBGSP) 2026: Fully Funded Master’s Scholarships for Developing Countries
summary: The Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program (JJ/WBGSP) is a highly competitive, fully funded scholarship supporting professionals from developing countries to pursue master’s degrees in development-related fields at leading universities worldwide. The program is administered by the World Bank and funded by the Government of Japan, with the objective of building a new generation of development leaders who will return to their home countries to contribute to social and economic progress. Overview of the JJ/WBGSP The JJ/WBGSP is open to citizens of eligible World Bank member developing countries who have relevant professional experience and a demonstrated commitment to development. For the 2026 academic year, subject to available funding, the program will support scholars enrolled in 44 participating master’s programs offered by 24 universities across the United States, Europe, Africa, Oceania, and Japan. Priority areas of study include, but are not limited to: Economic policy management Public finance and tax policy Infrastructure management Development economics Governance and public policy Applicants who are not admitted to one of the 44 participating master’s programs are not eligible to apply for the JJ/WBGSP. Application Windows for 2026 To accommodate different academic calendars, applications are organized into two rounds: Application Window#1 Application Window #1 from from January 15 to February 27, 2026 Application Window #2 Application Window #2 from March 30 to May 29, 2026 The JJ/WBGSP application link is only shared with candidates who: Have received unconditional admission (except for financing), and Have been shortlisted as eligible by a participating university Prospective applicants are advised to contact the university offering their target program to confirm whether they meet these requirements. Post-Study Commitment All JJ/WBGSP scholars are required to return to their home country after completing their studies and apply the knowledge and skills gained toward national development priorities. Scholarship Benefits A JJ/WBGSP scholarship provides tuition, a monthly living stipend, round-trip airfare, health insurance, and travel allowance. View a detailed list of benefits . The JJ/WBGSP provides comprehensive financial support, including: Round-trip economy airfare between the scholar’s home country and host university A travel allowance of USD 600 per trip Full tuition for the master’s program Basic medical insurance provided through the university A monthly living allowance to cover accommodation, food, books, and basic expenses The allowance amount varies by host country The scholarship covers the entire duration of the graduate program or up to two years , whichever is shorter. Costs Not Covered The scholarship does not cover: Visa application or residence permit fees Expenses for accompanying family members Computers or other educational equipment Language training not included in the degree program Additional travel during the study period Research costs, field trips, internships, or supplementary learning materials Workshops, seminars, or extracurricular activities The JJ/WBGSP may supplement, but does not duplicate, other financial awards. Any additional funding must be disclosed to the JJ/WBGSP Secretariat. Eligibility Criteria Eligibility requirements are strictly enforced and no exceptions are made. Applicants must: Be a national of a World Bank member developing country on this list ; Not hold dual citizenship of any developed country Be in good health Hold a bachelor’s degree earned at least three years before the application deadline Have at least three years of paid, full-time development-related employment acquired within the past six years Exceptions may apply for applicants from Fragile and Conflict-Affected States Be unconditionally admitted (except for funding) to a JJ/WBGSP participating master’s program located outside their country of citizenship and residence Upload an official letter of admission before submitting the scholarship application Applicants are not eligible if they: Are World Bank Group staff, Executive Directors, alternates, or close relatives of the above Previously declined a JJ/WBGSP scholarship Previously received a JJ/WBGSP scholarship but did not complete the degree Eligibility criteria do not change during an open application cycle. How to Apply The application process consists of two steps: Apply directly to one or more JJ/WBGSP participating master’s programs If admitted and shortlisted by the university, submit the JJ/WBGSP scholarship application using the link provided Applicants should consult the official Application Guidelines and FAQs for their respective application window. Selection Process Each eligible application is independently reviewed by two qualified assessors and scored on a scale of 1 to 10 based on the following criteria: Quality of professional experience (30 percent) Quality of professional recommendations (30 percent) Commitment to the applicant’s home country (30 percent) Educational background (10 percent) Final selection also considers geographic distribution, gender balance, financial need, and individual hardship. Approved finalists are confirmed by the JJ/WBGSP Steering Committee, and successful candidates are notified shortly thereafter. Why the JJ/WBGSP Is Important The Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship Program is designed to strengthen institutional and human capacity in developing countries by investing in skilled professionals who are positioned to influence policy, institutions, and development outcomes upon their return. IMPORTANT LINKS DEVELOPING COUNTRY NATIONALS WINDOW 1 ⇨ Application Guidelines ⇨ FAQ WINDOW 2 ⇨ Application Guidelines ⇨ FAQ JAPANESE NATIONALS ⇨ Application Guidelines ⇨ FAQ PARTICIPATING PROGRAMS ⇨ All Programs ⇨ Window 1 ⇨ Window 2 Announcements ⇨ The next application window for Japanese nationals will open from February 16 to April 17, 2026.
